howItWorks.intro: This doge federal spending cut impact by state calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Cut amount = federal spending to state × cut percentage
Per resident = cut amount / state population
Jobs at risk = federal-dependent jobs × cut percentage
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is DOGE?
faq.answer: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) is an initiative aimed at identifying and cutting wasteful federal spending. It has proposed cutting hundreds of billions from federal agencies, contracts, and programs.

faq.question: Which states are most dependent on federal spending?
faq.answer: States like West Virginia (2.4:1 ratio), Alaska (2.3:1), Mississippi (2.15:1), and New Mexico (2.1:1) receive the most federal dollars per tax dollar paid. These states would be disproportionately affected by across-the-board cuts.

faq.question: What federal spending goes to states?
faq.answer: The largest categories are Medicare/Medicaid (40%+), Social Security (25%+), military/defense (15%), and federal grants for infrastructure, education, and social programs (20%).

faq.question: Is the DOGE Federal Spending Cut Impact by State Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard geopolitics and energy markets formulas trusted by analysts and informed citizens. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the DOGE Federal Spending Cut Impact by State Calculator?
sections.body: The DOGE Federal Spending Cut Impact by State Calculator is a free online tool designed for analysts and informed citizens who need quick, accurate calculations in the geopolitics and energy markets space. By entering your your state, spending cut, you get instant results including federal $ to your state, amount cut, cut per resident. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting federal $ to your state right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In geopolitics and energy markets, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by analysts and informed citizens worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand global dynamics with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your your state and need to find the right federal $ to your state. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Your State
howToSteps.description: Type or select your your state in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Spending Cut (%)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your spending cut in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your federal $ to your state, amount cut, cut per resident. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your your state before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both federal $ to your state and amount cut — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your spending cut, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
